title_original: SEEQC Reports First Quantum Computer with Integrated Qubit Control on a Chip at Millikelvin Temperatures
summary_original: SEEQC and collaborators have demonstrated the first quantum computer system integrating superconducting digital control logic directly with qubits on a chip, all reliably operating at 10 millikelvin, as published in Nature Electronics. Researchers built a five-qubit processor stacked with a digital control chip, using Single Flux Quantum pulses to achieve single-qubit gate fidelities exceeding 99.5%, and ultra-low power dissipation measured in nanowatts per qubit. This architecture, validated through standard quantum benchmarking, significantly reduces wiring, thermal load, and system complexity compared to room-temperature control. SEEQC’s milestone addresses a critical scalability bottleneck in quantum computing, paving the way for chip-based systems with higher integration, improved manufacturability, and energy efficiency, essential for advancing beyond laboratory-scale quantum machines.
